summary: Matched the 312-byte common fighter HammerJump slice by moving six functions out of asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s into a new ftCo_HammerJump.c/.h pair, wiring the new object into obj_files.mk, and updating nearby ftCommon hammer-state users to include the new header. The matched functions cover the HammerJump transition helper, motion-state setup, and the Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll callbacks.
what_changed: Deleted the assembly implementations of ftCo_800C5A50, ftCo_800C5A98, ftCo_HammerJump_Anim, ftCo_HammerJump_IASA, ftCo_HammerJump_Phys, and ftCo_HammerJump_Coll from asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_HammerJump.c with matching C implementations for the HammerJump helper and callbacks.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_HammerJump.h declaring the six HammerJump functions with address comments.
Added ftCo_HammerJump.c.o to TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk before the remaining ft_0C31 asm object.
Moved HammerJump declarations out of src/melee/ft/ft_0C31.h into the new dedicated ftCo_HammerJump.h header.
Improved ftCo_800C5B88's prototype in ft_0C31.h from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void ftCo_800C5B88(HSD_GObj* gobj, enum_t).
Changed ftCo_800CAE80's declaration in ft_0C88.h from bool to enum_t, matching its use as a nonzero enum-like result passed to ftCo_800C5B88.
Updated HammerJump-dependent ftCommon files such as Bury, DamageIce, HammerTurn, HammerWait, HammerWalk, and Init to include ftCo_HammerJump.h directly.
smart_moves: Created a focused per-state header/source pair for ftCo_HammerJump instead of leaving matched symbols declared in the broad ft_0C31.h asm header.
Used typed forward declarations and real parameter types where the match exposed intent, especially replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for ftCo_800C5B88.
Recognized that ftCo_800CAE80 returns an enum-like value rather than a simple bool because ftCo_800C5A50 checks it for nonzero and passes it through to ftCo_800C5B88.
Kept thin callbacks simple: Anim delegates to ftCo_800C4F64, Phys delegates to ftCo_800CB438, Coll delegates to ftCo_HammerFall_Coll, and IASA is an empty function.
Preserved matching-sensitive local ordering in ftCo_800C5A98 with an explicit todo noting that flags cannot move below fp, suggesting an inline or compiler-ordering constraint.
decomp_lessons: When splitting a matched state out of an aggregate asm file, also remove the corresponding sdata2 constants from the asm if the new C object now owns them through generated literals.
A transition helper that returns bool may still depend on an enum return from a callee; avoid over-narrowing prototypes just because the caller tests for zero/nonzero.
For ftCommon state files, prefer dedicated state headers such as ftCo_HammerJump.h over central catch-all asm headers once symbols are decompiled.
Small state callback functions often match cleanly as one-call wrappers or empty functions; preserving that simplicity can be the correct matching tactic.
If a local variable placement is required for a match, document the ordering constraint in the C source rather than hiding it in unexplained rearrangements.
naming_conventions: Dedicated common fighter hammer-state files use the ftCo_HammerJump.c and ftCo_HammerJump.h naming pattern.
State callback names follow ftCo_HammerJump_Anim, ftCo_HammerJump_IASA, ftCo_HammerJump_Phys, and ftCo_HammerJump_Coll.
Unknown-address helper functions remain address-based names such as ftCo_800C5A50 and ftCo_800C5A98 when no semantic name is established.
Header guards in this slice use the address-style form GALE01_0C5A50.
Address comments in headers preserve symbol offsets, for example /* 0C5A50 */ before ftCo_800C5A50.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced 102 lines of PowerPC assembly in ft_0C31.s with C while preserving a 100% report for the 312-byte file.
Used a local enum_t variable in ftCo_800C5A50 to match the assembly pattern that calls ftCo_800CAE80, tests the result, passes it to ftCo_800C5B88, and returns true or false.
In ftCo_800C5A98, declared u32 flags before Fighter* fp because moving it below fp reportedly breaks the match.
Matched Fighter_ChangeMotionState with ftCo_MS_HammerJump, flags from ftCo_800C54C4, start frame from ftCo_800C548C(fp), rate 1, blend 0, and NULL.
Matched the IASA callback as an empty function and the Anim/Phys/Coll callbacks as direct wrappers around existing common hammer/fall helpers.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs renaming ftCo_800C5A50 or ftCo_800C5A98 to semantic HammerJump helper names.
Check whether the todo in ftCo_HammerJump.c about flags ordering and a possible inline was resolved in later decomp work.
Search subsequent PRs for further typing of ftCo_800C5B88 and ftCo_800CAE80.
Compare adjacent hammer-state matches such as ftCo_HammerWait, ftCo_HammerWalk, ftCo_HammerTurn, HammerKneeBend, and HammerFall for shared transition patterns.
